title: Writing - Part 1: Travel and Tourism
description: Your class has been discussing the advantages and disadvantages of international tourism.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the aspects of tourism in your notes. You should explain which aspect you think is more important to consider, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Tourism destroys the very places people travel to see.
Travelling broadens the mind and promotes understanding.
Local businesses rely on tourists to survive.

structure.introduction: Introduce international tourism and the two aspects you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first aspect (e.g., cultural exchange) and its value.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second aspect (e.g., environmental impact) and its consequences.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both aspects and state which you think is more important to consider.

content.modelAnswer:
International tourism is one of the world's largest industries, attracting billions of travellers each year. While it creates wonderful opportunities for discovery and economic growth, it also raises serious environmental questions. This essay will consider the benefits of cultural exchange and the environmental impact of mass tourism.

Travel enables people from different countries to meet, share experiences and develop mutual understanding. When tourists visit a foreign country, they experience new languages, foods and traditions. This exposure can break down stereotypes and foster greater global tolerance, making cultural exchange one of tourism's most valuable benefits.

Nevertheless, the environmental consequences of tourism cannot be ignored. Popular destinations often suffer from overcrowding, pollution and damage to natural habitats. The carbon footprint of international flights is particularly concerning, as air travel is one of the largest contributors to greenhouse gas emissions per passenger.

In conclusion, although cultural exchange is undeniably enriching, I believe the environmental impact of tourism is the more pressing concern today. Without careful management, the natural and cultural treasures that attract visitors risk being permanently damaged. Responsible and sustainable tourism must therefore become a global priority.
